source_metadata.title_raw: Julianna Tillquist - A-19-088 - June 26, 2019 - San Bernardino

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 26, 2019

Julianna Tillquisr
SBCTA Assistant General Counsel
1170 West Third Street
2nd Floor
San Bernardino, CA 92410

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-088

Dear Ms. Tillquisr:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the Act) and Section 1090.¹ Please note that we are only providing advice
under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act, and Section 1090, not under other general
conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest.

Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for
additional advice.

Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Bernardino
County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive
a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you
that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTIONS

Is San Bernardino County Transportation Authority (SBCTA) Engineer and Director of
Project Delivery and Toll Services Paula Beauchamp disqualified from taking part in governmental
decisions and contracts involving the California Department of Transportation (CalTrans), given
that her spouse is the CalTrans Director of District 8?

CONCLUSION

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-088
Page No. 2

Under the Act, Paula Beauchamp does not have a conflict of interest because her spouse's
government salary is not considered disqualifying "income." Per Section 1091.5(a)(6), Paula
Beauchamp also does not have a disqualifying financial interest in contracts between the two
agencies, given the lengthy history of her spouse's work for CalTrans.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

SBCTA is a county-wide government transportation agency. Paula Beauchamp works for
SBCTA as an Engineer and as the Director of Project Delivery and Toll Services. She has worked
in that capacity since January 8, 2017. Paula Beauchamp's spouse, Michael Beauchamp, a CalTrans
employee for over thirty years, was appointed CalTrans District 8 Director on February 20, 2019.
District 8 encompasses all of San Bernardino County.

Paula Beauchamp takes part in the decision-making process with respect to several
transportation projects under CalTrans oversight within District 8. Paula Beauchamp frequently
interacts with CalTrans District 8 representatives and periodically with the District 8 Director
regarding funding, environmental studies, design, right of way, and construction of transportation
projects that are under CalTrans' oversight. Paula Beauchamp's direct interactions with husband
Michael Beauchamp in their public official capacities may include the negotiation of cooperative
agreement terms between CalTrans and SBCTA regarding each entity's roles and responsibilities
with respect to the aforementioned projects, as well as discussions regarding partnering on projects
to maximize public improvements while minimizing costs and efforts. Cooperative agreements with
CalTrans are subject to SBCTA Board approval.

Additionally, Paula Beauchamp executes other documents either required by CalTrans or
subject to CalTrans approval, including geometric approval drawings, technical reports, right of
way certifications, special provisions, encroachment permits, contract acceptances, relief of
maintenance certifications, construction contract change orders, the SBCTA Quality Assurance
Manual, and environmental revalidations. Likewise, as an SBCTA Engineer, Paula Beauchamp
executes documents that are also executed by the CalTrans District 8 Director, such as project
reports, environmental documents, and various correspondence, all relating to projects under
CalTrans' oversight. In the future, these documents will have signature blocks for both Paula
Beauchamp, as SBCTA Director, and Michael Beauchamp, as CalTrans Director.

Some projects involve the state highway system and require SBCTA to acquire the right of
way necessary for construction, either through negotiated purchase or eminent domain, and to
acquire the right of way in SBCTA's name. At the conclusion of these projects, SBCTA conveys
the acquired right of way to CalTrans. The SBCTA Board has delegated authority to Paula
Beauchamp, as SBCTA Director, to execute SBCTA deeds conveying right of way. The CalTrans
District 8 Director signs certificates of acceptance of those deeds on behalf of CalTrans.

Paula Beauchamp's compensation is set by the SBCTA Executive Director within a pay
range established by the SBCTA Board of Directors, and is subject to merit increases granted at the
discretion of the Executive Director within Board-approved budgetary authorization. Michael
Beauchamp's compensation is set by the CalTrans Director or Chief Deputy Director within a pay
range established by the State for the District Director job classification.

File No. A-19-088
Page No. 3

ANALYSIS

The Act

Under the Political Reform Act, "[n]o public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest." (Section 87100.) Section 87103 provides, "[a] public official has a financial interest in a
decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official, a member of his or her immediate family," or on certain enumerated economic interests.
Among these enumerated economic interests is an interest in "[a]ny source of income, except gifts
or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or
more in value provided or promised to, or received by, the public official within 12 months prior to
the time when the decision is made." (Section 87103(c).)

Though the Act generally defines "income" to include "any community property interest in
the income of a spouse," income generally does not include "[s]alary and reimbursement for
expenses or per diem, and social security, disability, or other similar benefit payments received
from a state, local, or federal government agency ...." (Section 82030(a), (b).) Accordingly, Paula
Beauchamp does not have an economic interest in SBCTA, a local agency, as a source of income.
Similarly, under the Act, she does not have a source of income interest in CalTrans based on her
community property interest in her spouse's income. Assuming neither Paula Beauchamp nor
Michael Beauchamp has any other potential economic interest in any of the SBCTA projects
subject to CalTrans oversight, Paula Beauchamp has no disqualifying interest that would prevent
her from taking part in a governmental decision or decision-making process that involves CalTrans
oversight or interaction with her spouse in their capacities as public officials.

Section 1090

Apart from the Act, Section 1090 provides that public officials "shall not be financially
interested in any contract made by them in their official capacity, or by any body or board of which
they are members." A contract that violates Section 1090 is void, and the prohibition applies
regardless of whether the terms of the contract are fair and equitable to all parties. (Thomson v. Call
(1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 646-649.) However, within Section 1091.5–which identifies scenarios in
which a public official will be deemed to not have an interest in a contract–subdivision (a)(6)
provides that a public official has a non-interest in a contract if his or her interest is "[t]hat of a
spouse of an officer or employee of a public agency in his or her spouse's employment or
officeholding if his or her spouse's employment or officeholding has existed for at least one year
prior to his or her election or appointment."

The only interest Paula Beauchamp has in contracts between SBCTA and CalTrans, as
described above, stems from husband Michael Beauchamp's employment by CalTrans, i.e., the
source of income that husband's CalTrans employment provides. However, Michael Beauchamp
has been a CalTrans employee for over thirty years–that is, longer than a year before Paula

File No. A-19-088
Page No. 4

Beauchamp was appointed to her position—and, thus, Paula Beauchamp is deemed to not have an
interest in the contract, per Section 1091.5(a)(6).

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,


Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Kevin Cornwall
Counsel, Legal Division

KMC:aja

Project Delivery and Toll Services Paula Beauchamp disqualified from taking part in governmental 
decisions and contracts involving the California Department of Transportation (CalTrans), given 
that her spouse is the Cal Trans Director of District 8?

CONCLUSION: Under the Act, Paula Beauchamp does not have a conflict of interest because her spouse's 
government salary is not considered disqualifying "income." Per Section 1091.5(a)(6), Paula 
Beauchamp also does not have a disqualifying financial interest in contracts between the two 
agencies, given the lengthy history of her spouse's work for CalTrans.
